trustBrief.userJob: Understand why the Tools public artifact boundary matters before choosing the Sanchika proof route or a browser-local tool.
trustBrief.primaryDecision: Decide whether this proof surface explains a browser-local artifact boundary without implying saved workflow, compliance authority, or product-family visual uniformity.
trustBoundaries: Tools creates static browser-local artifacts only; Axal owns persistence, collaboration, audit trails, and recurring work.
Files are processed in your browser. No account or file upload required.
The public proof page must not send pasted rows or generated drafts through an application API.
evidenceRequirements: Source and provenance for every Sanchika claim remain visible and reviewable.
The generated artifact and its export boundary stay explicit before a visitor follows a tool route.
Browser evidence proves that desktop and mobile preserve the no-account and no-upload boundary.

claims.claim: ComplyEaze Tools is a static browser-local artifact surface with a separately reviewed Sanchika proof route.
claims.evidence: The existing public route, source repository, static CSP, and this consumer run identify the boundary and pending S11 proof.

nonGoals: No product spec is inferred from a design-system page.
No generic route or tool scaffold is introduced before a selected direction completes the owner gate.
No backend, account, upload, telemetry, remote worker, persisted artifact, or compliance decision is in scope.

designBrief.firstViewportSignal: ComplyEaze Tools makes browser-local artifact boundaries and the Sanchika proof route understandable before a visitor is asked to leave the page.
designBrief.emotionalIntent: Practical confidence: a clear local workbench for review-minded people, not a design-system shrine or generic SaaS sales page.
narrativeArc: Show how source, proof, and export remain distinct.
Give a precise next route without promising stored workflow or compliance judgment.
responsiveConstraints: Mobile keeps the boundary, source link, and next action in one reading order without horizontal overflow.
Desktop separates local artifact work from the Sanchika proof without importing Sanchika identity geometry.
nonGoals: No product spec, dashboard shell, fake artifact evidence, Sanchika wordmark transfer, generated imagery, or motion-gated content.

disqualificationReasons: Trust veto: a plausible ZIP filename violates the Tools no-committed-filename boundary.
No recognition proxy was retained for this review round.

vetoes.reason: The plausible ZIP filename weakens the Tools synthetic and no-file-custody boundary.

reconciliation.subject: tools-sanchika-public consumer build
reconciliation.reason: The approved Field Guide direction was implemented only in the isolated Tools worktree. Its browser-local boundary, source links, and Axal persistence handoff remain explicit in the retained local build evidence.

renderEvidence.finding: Isolated plain-request comparison control at 1440px; it is not reviewed adoption evidence.
Isolated plain-request comparison control at 390px; it is not reviewed adoption evidence.
Accessibility acceptance remains a required post-selection gate; this shape record prevents premature claims.
Browser-verified local implementation at 1440×900 with the boundary adjacent to the first-viewport proof copy.
Browser-verified local implementation at 390×844 with the browser-local boundary before outbound proof actions.
Repository, browser-semantic, keyboard, responsive, and static-network verification; not user validation or production evidence.

adoptionEvidence.verificationRun: Local implementation passed pnpm verify, policy scans, and browser verification against the built static route at 1440×900 and 390×844.
adoptionEvidence.rollbackPlan: Retain the live e1993cb5b23f359a0afe3d98c57d3693d4048bee Tools route as the rollback baseline until a reviewed replacement is deployed.

residualRisks: Production approval, merged-source evidence, cold-cache measurements, rollback verification, and post-deploy smoke have not occurred.
The current local build links to source rather than claiming per-tool source, supported-input, or review-date metadata.
nextActions: Complete current-head review and CI for Tools PR #90 at 9f1aa72, then retain merged-source evidence before requesting production approval.

nextAction: Complete current-head review and CI for Tools PR #90 at 9f1aa72, retain merged-source evidence, then request the separate production approval.
